spec.reasoning: The task is a lookup/match across supplier sheets and a calculation sheet. I renamed columns consistently across all sheets: 'Item '/'ITEM' -> 'Prod_No'/'PROD_NO', 'Name'/'NAME' -> 'Descr'/'DESCR', 'PL-code'/'PL-Code' -> 'Cat-code'/'Cat-Code', 'Price'/'PRICE' -> 'Cost'/'COST', 'Discount' -> 'Rebate', 'My price' -> 'My cost'. All renames maintain the same descriptiveness level (abbreviation to abbreviation, full word to full word). Sheet names are renamed from Supplier_X to Vendor_X and Calc to Summary, Discount_S1 to Rebate_V1. Each old name maps to a unique new name, avoiding the collision issue from previous attempts. A distractor column 'Region' is added to Vendor_1 to test whether the agent incorrectly uses positional assumptions. The instruction references specific cell addresses (A10, B7) and the concept of 'supplier' which is a general English word in the instruction, so I renamed sheets from Supplier to Vendor but did not rename the word 'supplier' in the instruction (instruction_paraphrase is false). The column 'NAME'/'Name' is renamed to 'DESCR'/'Descr' — the instruction says 'name of a supplier' using 'name' as general English, but the column 'NAME' is a column header reference; however since instruction_paraphrase is false, the instruction text stays unchanged.
